NIHR award information

- Scheme: https://www.nihr.ac.uk/career-development/research-career-funding-programmes/postdoctoral/postdoctoral-fellowships

- Guidance: https://www.nihr.ac.uk/research-funding/guidance/domestic-personal-career-development-award-research-funding

- Specific proposal guidance: https://www.nihr.ac.uk/research-funding/guidance/domestic-personal-career-development-award-research-funding#tab-396436

- Narrative CV guidance: https://www.nihr.ac.uk/research-funding/guidance/domestic-personal-career-development-award-research-funding#tab-396381

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## NIHR key dates (Cohort 3)

- **23 Apr 2026** --- application portal opens
- **16 Jun 2026, 1pm** --- submission deadline (hard; late submissions rejected)
- **Jun--Dec 2026** --- application assessments
- **Jan--Mar 2027** --- interviews; funding decisions
- **Apr--Sep 2027** --- award start dates

## Application development milestones

Required uploads --- allow time to prepare each:

- Narrative CV
- Detailed research plan
- Gantt chart / project plan
- Career development programme
- Letters of support (contracting organisation; partner organisation if applicable)
- References
- SoECAT (if applicable)

Dependencies that can block submission:

- Head(s) of Department must confirm participation in the awards management system before you can submit
- Mentors must confirm participation in the system before you can submit
- Partner organisation letter needed if employer ≠ contracting organisation, or if salaried clinical/practice time will not be at contracting organisation

Suggested internal deadlines (working back from 16 Jun 2026):

- Identify contracting organisation and mentors → as early as possible
- Draft research plan and Gantt chart → by \~mid-Apr 2026
- Send support letter requests → by \~late Apr 2026 (allow 4+ weeks)
- Complete narrative CV → by \~early May 2026
- All parties confirm participation in system → by \~9 Jun 2026
- Final review and submission → by \~13 Jun 2026 (3 days buffer)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## What can be funded

### Your salary

- 50--100% FTE; duration 2--5 years FTE (your choice)
- Funded at **80% FEC** if hosted at an HEI (direct + indirect costs)
- Funded at **100%** if hosted at NHS body, local authority, charity/NGO, or independent research organisation
- Include internal incremental salary increases in costings; exclude nationally agreed pay increases
- Disclose full salary and on-costs --- summarised charge-out or daily rates not accepted

### Support post (verbatim from scheme, Cohort 3 overview)

> "a support post to assist your research, plus £3,000 towards their training and development"

- One support post per award
- £3,000 ring-fenced for that person's training and development; cannot be moved to other cost categories
- "Shared staff costs" also covered as a separate line item
- Subject to same 80%/100% FEC rate as above depending on host organisation
- General staff rule (finance guidance): "If there are any applicant(s) whose costs are not being claimed, you should still include their details within this section, but at zero cost."
  --- applies to all staff; not stated specifically for support post

### Funding rates by host organisation (career development funding)

  | Host                     | Direct costs | Indirect costs |
  | ------------------------ | ------------ | -------------- |
  | HEI                      | 80% FEC      | 80% FEC        |
  | NHS body                 | 100%         | 0%             |
  | Local authority          | 100%         | 100%           |
  | Charity / NGO            | 100%         | 100%           |
  | Independent research org | 100%         | 100%           |

### Training and development (for you)

- Funded at up to **100%** --- separate cost category, cannot be moved to other categories
- Includes travel and subsistence for training activities
- NIHR covers HEI PhD fees at standard UK rate (not international rate)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
